questions.question: Where was Lencho's house located?

options.B: On the crest of a low hill

questions.explanation: The text states the house sat on the crest of a low hill and was the only one in the entire valley.

questions.question: What could one see from the height of Lencho's house?

options.C: The river and the field of ripe corn

questions.explanation: From that height, one could see the river and the field of ripe corn dotted with flowers.

questions.question: What did the earth need according to Lencho?

options.C: A downpour or at least a shower

questions.explanation: The story mentions that the only thing the earth needed was a downpour or at least a shower.

questions.question: Towards which direction did Lencho look throughout the morning?
questions.explanation: Lencho had done nothing else but see the sky towards the north-east throughout the morning.

questions.question: What did Lencho compare the big drops of rain to?
questions.explanation: Lencho exclaimed that the big drops were ten cent pieces and the little ones were fives.

questions.question: What began to fall along with the rain suddenly?
questions.explanation: Suddenly a strong wind began to blow and along with the rain very large hailstones began to fall.

questions.question: What did the hailstones resemble in the eyes of the boys?
questions.explanation: The text notes that the hailstones 'truly did resemble new silver coins'.

questions.question: How long did the hail rain on the entire valley?
questions.explanation: The story states, 'For an hour the hail rained on the house... on the whole valley'.

questions.question: After the storm, the field looked as if it were covered with _______.
questions.explanation: The field was white, as if covered with salt.

questions.question: What was the effect of the hailstorm on the corn field?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly says, 'The corn was totally destroyed'.

questions.question: What did Lencho say would have left more than the hailstorm?
questions.explanation: Lencho told his sons, 'A plague of locusts would have left more than this'.

questions.question: What was the single hope left in the hearts of Lencho's family?
questions.explanation: In their hearts, there was a single hope: help from God.

questions.question: Lencho was described as an 'ox of a man'. What does this mean?

options.B: He was very strong and hardworking

questions.explanation: The expression means he worked like an animal (very hard) in the fields.

questions.question: On which day did Lencho begin to write the letter to God?
questions.explanation: The following Sunday, at daybreak, he began to write a letter.

questions.question: How much money did Lencho ask for in his letter?
questions.explanation: He wrote that he needed a hundred pesos to sow his field again and to live until the crop comes.

questions.question: What address did Lencho write on the envelope?
questions.explanation: He wrote 'To God' on the envelope.

questions.question: The postmaster was a fat and _______ fellow.
questions.explanation: The text describes the postmaster as a fat, amiable fellow.

questions.question: Why did the postmaster decide to answer the letter?

options.C: In order not to shake the writer's faith in God
options.D: To get a reward from God

questions.explanation: In order not to shake the writer’s faith in God, the postmaster came up with the idea to answer the letter.

questions.question: What did the postmaster need to answer the letter besides goodwill, ink, and paper?
questions.explanation: When he opened it, it was evident that to answer it he needed something more: money (the hundred pesos Lencho requested).

questions.question: How much money was the postmaster able to collect?

options.C: A little more than half (70 pesos)

questions.explanation: He was able to send the farmer only a little more than half, which was seventy pesos.

questions.question: What signature did the postmaster use on the reply letter?
questions.explanation: He put the money in an envelope and with it a letter containing only a single word as a signature: God.

questions.question: Was Lencho surprised to find the money in the letter?
questions.explanation: Lencho showed not the slightest surprise on seeing the money; such was his confidence.

questions.question: Why did Lencho become angry after counting the money?

options.B: The amount was less than he asked for

questions.explanation: He became angry because he received only seventy pesos instead of the hundred he requested.

questions.question: In his second letter, what did Lencho call the post office employees?
questions.explanation: Lencho warned God not to send the rest through the mail because the employees are 'a bunch of crooks'.

questions.question: Who is the author of 'A Letter to God'?
questions.explanation: The story is written by G.L. Fuentes.

questions.question: What does the word 'crest' mean in the context of the story?

options.C: The top or highest part of a hill

questions.explanation: Glossary defines crest as the top or highest part of a hill.

questions.question: According to the story, what are 'locusts'?

options.B: Insects which fly in big groups and destroy crops

questions.explanation: Glossary defines locusts as insects which fly in big groups and destroy crops.

questions.question: What is the currency of several Latin American countries mentioned in the story?
questions.explanation: The currency mentioned and defined in the glossary is the peso.

questions.question: What does 'conscience' refer to in the text?

options.C: An inner sense of right and wrong

questions.explanation: Glossary notes conscience as an inner sense of right and wrong.

questions.question: What does the word 'amiable' mean?
questions.explanation: Glossary defines amiable as friendly and pleasant.

questions.question: A violent tropical storm in which strong winds move in a circle is called a _______.
questions.explanation: This description matches the definition of a cyclone in the vocabulary section.

questions.question: An extremely strong wind is known as a _______.
questions.explanation: The vocabulary section identifies a gale as an extremely strong wind.

questions.question: A violent storm whose centre is a cloud in the shape of a funnel is a _______.
questions.explanation: This matches the definition of a tornado in the vocabulary section.

questions.question: Why did Lencho's soul fill with sadness after the storm?
questions.explanation: The destruction of the corn meant no food for the year, filling him with sorrow.

questions.question: What did the postmaster give as part of the charity?
questions.explanation: The postmaster himself gave part of his salary for the act of charity.

questions.question: How did Lencho affix the stamp to the second envelope?

options.B: With a blow of his fist

questions.explanation: He licked the stamp and then affixed it with a blow of his fist.

questions.question: In the sentence 'I hope it passes quickly', what part of speech is 'hope'?
questions.explanation: In this context, 'hope' is used as a verb expressing a wish.

questions.question: Lencho compared the raindrops to coins. What did the little ones represent?
questions.explanation: He said the big drops are ten cent pieces and the little ones are fives.

questions.question: Where was G.L. Fuentes from?
questions.explanation: Gregorio Lopez Fuentes was a Mexican novelist.

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'drape' as used in the story?
questions.explanation: The text describes the field 'draped' in a curtain of rain, meaning covered.

questions.question: What was the result of Lencho's second letter regarding the remaining money?

options.C: The story ends before we see the result

questions.explanation: The story ends with the postmaster reading Lencho's second letter; the final outcome is left to the reader's imagination.

questions.question: What did the field of ripe corn always promise?
questions.explanation: The corn field dotted with flowers 'always promised a good harvest'.

questions.question: How did Lencho know his fields?
questions.explanation: The text states Lencho 'knew his fields intimately'.

questions.question: What was the 'frozen pearls' a metaphor for?
questions.explanation: The boys ran out to collect the hailstones, which the narrator calls 'frozen pearls'.

questions.question: What did the postman do when he first saw the letter addressed to God?

options.C: He went to his boss laughing heartily
options.D: He delivered it to the church

questions.explanation: The postman showed the letter to his boss while laughing because he had never known that address.

questions.question: What quality of Lencho's did the postmaster wish he had?
questions.explanation: The postmaster commented, 'What faith! I wish I had the faith of the man who wrote this letter'.

questions.question: What was the postmaster's 'resolution'?

options.B: To answer Lencho's letter and provide money
options.D: To report Lencho to the police

questions.explanation: He stuck to his resolution to answer the letter by collecting money for an act of charity.

questions.question: When did Lencho return to the post office to check for a reply?
questions.explanation: The following Sunday Lencho came a bit earlier than usual to ask if there was a letter.

questions.question: What was the postmaster experiencing while looking on from his office?
questions.explanation: He was 'experiencing the contentment of a man who has performed a good deed'.

questions.question: Why did Lencho use the public writing-table for his second letter?

options.A: He had no paper at home
options.B: It's where people usually wrote their letters at the post office

questions.explanation: As part of the post office facilities, he used the 'public writing-table' to express his ideas.

questions.question: What caused the 'much wrinkling' of Lencho's brow while writing?

options.C: The effort he had to make to express his ideas

questions.explanation: The wrinkling was 'caused by the effort he had to make to express his ideas'.

questions.question: According to Lencho's belief, God's eyes see _______.

options.B: Everything, even what is deep in one's conscience
options.C: Only what is written in letters

questions.explanation: Lencho had been instructed that God's eyes see everything, even deep within the conscience.

questions.question: The phrase 'an act of charity' refers to _______.

options.B: The postmaster's effort to help Lencho

questions.explanation: The postmaster asked for contributions from staff and friends for 'an act of charity' for Lencho.

questions.question: Which of these words describes a 'dishonest person' in the glossary?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'crooks' as dishonest persons.

questions.question: G.L. Fuentes was awarded the National Prize of Arts and Science in _______.
questions.explanation: According to the 'About the Author' section, he received the award in 1935.

questions.question: What is the CURRENCY name used in the story?
questions.explanation: Lencho asks for 100 'pesos'.

questions.question: How did Lencho feel throughout the night after his crops were destroyed?

options.B: He thought only of one hope: the help of God
options.C: He planned to leave the valley
options.D: He was angry at his family

questions.explanation: The text states, 'All through the night, Lencho thought only of one hope: the help of God'.

questions.question: The 'older boys' were _______ while the rain started.
questions.explanation: The older boys were working in the field while the smaller ones played.

questions.question: In the story, 'solitary' means _______.
questions.explanation: The glossary defines solitary as lonely or single.

questions.question: What does 'dotted with' mean?
questions.explanation: Glossary: 'dotted with - scattered over an area'.

questions.question: What was the 'pleasure' Lencho went out for when the rain started?

options.B: Feeling the rain on his body

questions.explanation: He went out for 'no other reason than to have the pleasure of feeling the rain on his body'.

questions.question: Lencho was a _______.
questions.explanation: Lencho is a farmer who writes a letter to God when his crops fail.

questions.question: What happened to the flowers after the hailstorm?

options.B: They were gone from the plants

questions.explanation: The story says, 'The flowers were gone from the plants'.

questions.question: How many paragraphs describe the story before the first glossary?
questions.explanation: The narrative spans several pages before the comprehension exercises.

questions.question: The second letter from Lencho asked God to _______.

options.B: Send the rest of the money

questions.explanation: He asks God to 'Send me the rest since I need it very much'.

questions.question: What 'always promised a good harvest'?

options.B: The field of ripe corn dotted with flowers

questions.explanation: The field of ripe corn dotted with flowers 'always promised a good harvest'.

questions.question: Why did Lencho not want the money sent through the mail?

options.B: He thought the employees would steal it again
options.C: The post office was too far

questions.explanation: He called the employees 'a bunch of crooks' and asked God not to send it through the mail.

questions.question: Lencho predicted the rain during _______.
questions.explanation: It was during the meal that big drops of rain began to fall as predicted.

questions.question: The word 'upset' in the glossary is defined as _______.
questions.explanation: Glossary: 'upset : disturbed'.

questions.question: When did Lencho's first letter reach the town?

options.D: The text doesn't specify the arrival time, only when he wrote it

questions.explanation: He wrote it Sunday at daybreak and then 'went to town' to place it in the mail.

questions.question: What does G.L. Fuentes' writing often relate to?
questions.explanation: His writing relates to the Mexican Revolution and civil conflict.

questions.question: How did the air feel when the rain began?
questions.explanation: The text says 'The air was fresh and sweet'.

questions.question: What is 'predict' in the glossary?
questions.explanation: Glossary: 'predict : foretell the future'.

questions.question: Lencho stood 'in the middle of the field' after _______.
questions.explanation: When the storm had passed, he stood in the middle of the field to talk to his sons.

questions.question: The postmaster turned serious and tapped the letter on his _______.
questions.explanation: He turned serious and was 'tapping the letter on his desk'.

questions.question: What did the postmaster experience as he watched Lencho get the letter?
questions.explanation: He experienced the contentment of performing a good deed.

questions.question: Lencho asked for 100 pesos. He received _______.
questions.explanation: In the letter he writes: 'only seventy pesos reached me'.

questions.question: What did Lencho think of God's capacity for error?

options.A: He thought God was very forgetful
options.B: He thought God could not have made a mistake
options.C: He thought God was testing him

questions.explanation: The text says, 'God could not have made a mistake'.

questions.question: Which word describes a firm decision in the glossary?
questions.explanation: Glossary: 'resolution : a firm decision'.

questions.question: The theme of the story 'A Letter to God' is primarily about _______.
questions.explanation: The story revolves around Lencho's absolute faith that God will help him when he asks.
